channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: AI Consultants
executive_summary: Nate B Jones emphasizes that successful AI consultants combine deep domain expertise with specific AI knowledge, avoiding vague pitches that dilute credibility. He highlights the importance of existing client relationships for scaling, the need for clear communication about AI applications, and the value of publishing insights to stand out in a crowded market.
bullets: Consultants who already own client relationships can scale fastest, according to Nate B Jones. This means that if you have a solid client base, you can more easily introduce AI solutions that genuinely add value. It's like having a garden where the plants are already thriving; you can just add new flowers without starting from scratch.

Buzzword-heavy pitches like 'AI-powered everything' can destroy credibility. Clients are looking for clear, technically sound answers, such as knowing when to use RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and why. Think of it like a chef who claims to make the best dish but can’t explain the ingredients; nobody will trust their cooking.

Owning a specific domain allows you to wrap AI around deep sector expertise, similar to how a strangler fig grows around a tree. This strategy helps you establish niche authority and increases your pricing power as you become the go-to expert in that area. Just like a fig tree that thrives by supporting its host, your AI skills will flourish within your domain.

AI enables templates to evolve in real-time, making services sharply scoped yet highly customizable for each engagement. This means you can adapt your offerings on the fly, ensuring they meet the unique needs of each client. It's like having a tailor who can adjust a suit while you wait, rather than making you come back for multiple fittings.

Building a trusted network is crucial because no single consultant can cover the entire value chain. Cultivating specialist partners allows you to bring the right expertise to every project, ensuring comprehensive solutions. Think of it like assembling a sports team where each player has a specific role; together, you can win the game.

Publishing detailed, NDA-safe insights publicly can help you separate yourself from generic 'AI consultants.' This transparency builds credibility and fuels referrals, making you a trusted source in the industry. It’s like a lighthouse guiding ships safely to shore; your insights help clients navigate the murky waters of AI.

Nate B Jones states, 'We don’t need more AI slogans; we need consultants who can answer, ‘When should I use RAG—and why?’ This highlights the need for consultants to provide substantive, actionable advice rather than just flashy marketing. It's akin to a doctor who gives a diagnosis instead of just handing out prescriptions without explanation.

A strangler-fig strategy works effectively, meaning you should grow your AI expertise around the trunk of the domain you already dominate. This approach allows you to leverage your existing knowledge while expanding into AI. Think of it like a tree that grows stronger as it wraps around a sturdy pillar.

If you engage in AI-washing today, you risk losing not just the deal but also poisoning the ecosystem for tomorrow. This means that overhyping your AI capabilities can lead to long-term distrust in the industry. Imagine a town that gets a reputation for bad food; even if a new restaurant opens, people will be hesitant to try it.

Focusing on a narrow niche allows you to articulate concrete deliverables clearly. This specificity not only helps in client retention but also allows you to command higher fees for your expertise. It's like a specialized doctor who charges more because they are the best in their field, rather than a general practitioner.

Consultants should aim to create living templates that can adapt as projects evolve. This flexibility ensures that your services remain relevant and tailored to client needs, which is crucial in a fast-paced AI landscape. Think of it like a smartphone app that updates automatically to improve user experience without requiring a complete overhaul.

To build a successful AI consulting practice, you must leverage an existing book of business or create one through clear, public content that showcases your expertise. This strategy helps you attract clients who are looking for knowledgeable consultants. It’s like a magnet that draws in metal shavings; your content pulls in potential clients seeking your specific skills.
key_takeaways: DO leverage your existing client relationships to scale your AI consulting practice. This is crucial because established trust can accelerate your growth in a competitive market. If you don’t, you might struggle to gain traction against more experienced consultants.

DO avoid AI-washing in your pitches. Instead, provide clear, actionable insights about when and how to use AI tools like RAG. Failing to do this can lead to a loss of credibility and trust with potential clients.

DO focus on a specific domain where you can develop deep expertise. This will help you command higher fees and establish yourself as a niche authority. If you don’t, you risk being seen as just another generic consultant in a crowded field.

DO publish detailed insights that are NDA-safe to build credibility and attract referrals. This transparency will help you stand out from generic AI consultants. If you skip this, you may blend into the background and miss out on valuable opportunities.

DO create living templates that can evolve with each client engagement. This adaptability will ensure your services remain relevant and tailored, which is essential in the fast-paced AI landscape. If you don’t, you risk offering outdated solutions that don’t meet client needs.

technical_terms.RAG: Retrieval-Augmented Generation, a method that combines retrieval of information with generative AI to provide more accurate and contextually relevant responses. Think of it like a librarian who not only finds books for you but also summarizes the key points you need.
technical_terms.AI-washing: The practice of overhyping or misrepresenting AI capabilities in marketing, leading to a loss of credibility. Think of it like a magician who claims to perform real magic but is just using sleight of hand; eventually, the audience will catch on.
technical_terms.Living templates: Dynamic frameworks that can adapt and evolve in real-time based on client needs and project requirements. Think of it like a chameleon that changes color to blend into its environment; it remains relevant no matter the situation.
technical_terms.Domain expertise: Deep knowledge and understanding of a specific industry or field, which enhances the ability to provide tailored AI solutions. Think of it like a seasoned sailor who knows every current and tide in a particular harbor; they can navigate more effectively than a novice.
technical_terms.Value chain: The full range of activities that businesses engage in to bring a product or service from conception to delivery. Think of it like a relay race where each runner has a specific leg to complete; the team’s success depends on how well each runner performs their part.
